name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: Citizens United opened the floodgates for dark money that is corroding our democracy. We need to overturn it as soon as possible to put power back in the hands of the people. #S1TownHall

tweet: Most of the money spent on our politics now comes from wealthy white men. If we get big money out of politics and give every voter Democracy Dollars, we can make sure Black and Brown voters have an equal voice. #S1TownHall

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: Dark money comes from organizations that aren't required to disclose their donors, making it impossible to know the special and corporate interests trying to influence us. We need the #ForThePeople Act to weed out dark money and bring accountability and transparency. #S1TownHall

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: The #ForThePeople Act doesn't just get big money out of politics — it also protects the right to vote and fights voter suppression. We need to do all three to make sure voters' voices are heard. #S1TownHall

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: It all comes back to dark money. When corporations can donate unlimited sums of money to organizations that are not required to disclose their donors, then voters and elected officials don't know who is trying to influence their votes. #S1TownHall

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: The fight to end gun violence is a good example of how this happens. When the NRA wants to shut down a bill they don't like, they can threaten to run ads against elected officials who don't oppose it, or funnel money to other candidates who promise they will. #S1TownHall

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: First, we need to pass the #ForThePeople Act to get big money out of politics. Next, I've proposed upgrading our public financing system to give every voter Democracy Dollars to support their favorite candidates — giving every voter a chance to make their voice heard. #S1TownHall

name: Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand
tweet: Behind every supposedly "unfixable" problem in D.C. — from gun violence, to climate change, to health care — is the corrupting influence of Big Money. A small number of extremely wealthy donors and well-funded PACs can drown out the voices of voters. Here's how we can fix it ➡
